---
title: "Calculating TWI/TPI from DEMs"
lang: en
---

This site summarizes how to calculate the Topographic Wetness Index (TWI) and Topographic Position Index (TPI) from DEMs (digital elevation models), mainly with R.

::: {.callout-note}
[Japanese version](../index.qmd)
:::

## Notebooks

The notebooks cover the workflow from preparing DEM data to calculating TWI and TPI.
Use the **English** tab in the navigation bar, or the links below.

- [Preparing DEM data](notebooks/dem_download.qmd)
- [Calculating the Topographic Wetness Index (TWI) with R](notebooks/topographic_wetness_index.qmd)
- [Calculating the Topographic Position Index (TPI) with R](notebooks/topographic_position_index.qmd)
- [Calculating TWI and TPI with QGIS](notebooks/topographic_wetness_index_qgis.qmd)

## App

The site also includes a conceptual TWI demo built with [Shinylive](https://shiny.posit.co/py/get-started/shinylive.html), which runs a Shiny app on a static website.
Use **Shinylive App** in the navigation bar, or open the link below.

- [Conceptual TWI demo](../app/)

Running WhiteboxTools in Shinylive appears to be difficult, so this is not a full application.

A separate hosted application is available for calculating TWI and TPI from your own DEM or sample data.
Use **TWI/TPI calculation app** in the navigation bar, or open the link below.

- [TWI/TPI calculation app](https://maple60-dem-terrain-indices-app.share.connect.posit.cloud/)

The application runs on [Posit Connect Cloud](https://posit.co/products/enterprise/connect-cloud).
Its source code is available here:

- [maple60/dem-terrain-indices-app](https://github.com/maple60/dem-terrain-indices-app)

## Other Notes

To view or run the code locally, use the GitHub icon in the upper-right corner to open the repository.

This repository uses [renv](https://rstudio.github.io/renv/) for R package management.
After cloning the repository, run the following command in an R console to install the required packages.

```{.r}
# install.packages("renv") # if renv is not installed yet
renv::restore()
```
